meta.title: Nokka — Screen Recording for Mac
meta.description: Nokka is a free, native macOS screen recorder. Record your full screen, a specific window, or any region with system audio. Local MP4 files. No account, no cloud, no complexity.

hero.headline: Your screen. Your recording. Your Mac.
hero.subheadline: A free, native macOS screen recorder. Capture your full screen, a window, or any region — with system audio. Local files, no account, no cloud.

ownIt.title: Own It
ownIt.description: Every recording saves directly to your Mac as an MP4. No cloud uploads, no account required, no platform that can revoke access. You participated in it — you should own it.

controlIt.title: Control It
controlIt.description: Choose your recording mode: the full screen, a specific window, or drag to select any region. Record what you intend to. Nothing more.

forgetIt.title: Forget It
forgetIt.description: Nokka records in the background with a minimal floating bar. Switch apps, browse docs, write code — the recording captures exactly what you told it to, while you do everything else.

fullScreen.label: Full Screen
fullScreen.title: Everything. All of it.
fullScreen.description: One click captures your entire display at full resolution. The simplest way to record — no decisions, no setup. Just your screen, exactly as you see it.

regionSelect.label: Region Select
regionSelect.title: Draw a rectangle. Record what’s inside.
regionSelect.description: Click and drag to define your recording area. Resize with handles, see dimensions in real-time. Perfect for tutorials, specific app sections, or social media dimensions.

windowCapture.label: Window Capture
windowCapture.title: Pick a window. Any window.
windowCapture.description: Select a specific application window from a visual grid — even if it’s in another Space or running fullscreen. Record that app while you work in another.
bullets: Works across Spaces and fullscreen apps

featuresGrid.heading: Everything you need. Nothing you don’t.

systemAudio.title: System Audio + Mic
systemAudio.description: Capture system sound and your microphone on separate tracks. Record what you hear and what you say.

pauseResume.title: Pause & Resume
pauseResume.description: Life interrupts. Pause your recording, handle it, pick up exactly where you left off.

globalShortcuts.title: Global Shortcuts
globalShortcuts.description: ⌘⇧R to record. ⌘⇧P to pause. ⌘⇧M for mic. Works from anywhere, no window switching.

menuBarApp.title: Menu Bar App
menuBarApp.description: Lives in your menu bar. Click to start. No dock icon, no window clutter, no setup ritual.

floatingControlBar.title: Floating Control Bar
floatingControlBar.description: A minimal pill-shaped bar stays visible during recording. Minimizable when you don’t need it.

countdownTimer.title: Countdown Timer
countdownTimer.description: Configurable countdown before recording starts. Enough time to switch to the right app.

h264.title: H.264 Hardware Acceleration
h264.description: Hardware-accelerated MP4 encoding. Smooth recording without taxing your CPU.

macosNative.title: macOS Native
macosNative.description: Built with SwiftUI and ScreenCaptureKit. No Electron, no web wrapper. Feels like it belongs on your Mac.

loom.name: vs. Loom
loom.description: Loom is great for sharing recordings with a team. But it requires an account, uploads everything to the cloud, and you can’t pause mid-recording. If you want local files and more recording control, Nokka is simpler.

obs.name: vs. OBS
obs.description: OBS is a professional broadcasting tool. It can do almost anything — if you’re willing to learn it. For quick screen recordings on your Mac, it’s like driving a semi truck to the grocery store.

quicktime.name: vs. QuickTime
quicktime.description: QuickTime is built in and free. But it can’t record system audio without workarounds, doesn’t support region selection, and the controls are buried in File menus. It works until it doesn’t.

builtIn.name: vs. macOS Built-in (⌘⇧5)
builtIn.description: The built-in toolbar is convenient but limited. No system audio capture, basic controls, no pause/resume. Fine for a quick screenshot. Not enough for real recording work.

downloadCta.heading: Ready to record?
downloadCta.subheading: Download Nokka and start recording in seconds.
downloadCta.requirements: macOS 14 Sonoma or later · Apple Silicon & Intel · ~5 MB
